Strategy.text: How is Sustainability reflected in your overall corporate strategy?
Strategy.option_a: I am not aware that Sustainability is part of our corporate strategy
Strategy.option_b: There is a rather short mention of Sustainability in our strategy
Strategy.option_c: We do consider Sustainability in our strategy and we are working to reflect it better
Strategy.option_d: Sustainability is one of the supporting elements in our strategy and there is some detail behind it
Strategy.option_e: Sustainability is a core element of our strategy and critical for the existence and growth of our business

Strategy.text: How is Sustainability reflected in the values of your organisation?
Strategy.option_b: We care but do not explicitly consider it as a differentiating value
Strategy.option_c: We are planning to make Sustainability one of our corporate values
Strategy.option_d: Sustainability is one of our values
Strategy.option_e: Sustainability is one of our core values and we monitor how it is exhibited in our culture on a regular basis

Strategy.text: Is there board-level oversight of climate-related issues within your organisation and how does this operate?
Strategy.option_b: There is limited oversight and we consider climate-related issues in our board discussions from time to time
Strategy.option_c: Our board actively discusses (and tackles) climate-related issues
Strategy.option_d: Yes, they review climate-related issues at board level on a regular basis
Strategy.option_e: Yes, there is a board member who has responsibility for progressing climate-related issues and we have a solid agenda for it

Strategy.text: Does your organisation have a process for identifying, assessing, and responding to climate-related risks and opportunities?
Strategy.option_b: We are working on developing a structured and standard process
Strategy.option_c: Yes, we have a basic process in place that needs improvement
Strategy.option_d: Yes, we have a process in place and we follow it on as needed basis
Strategy.option_e: Yes, we have a mature and detailed process in place that is regularly reviewed and updated

Strategy.text: Do you benchmark your Sustainability progress against your industry peers?
Strategy.option_b: We are currently considering performing a benchmarking study
Strategy.option_c: Yes, we have done it in the past, but it is not a scheduled event
Strategy.option_d: Yes, we do it, but it is done on an ad hoc basis when requested by our CEO or the board
Strategy.option_e: Yes, we conduct a benchmarking exercise at least every 6-12 months

Strategy.text: What is your CDP score (Climate Change)?
Strategy.option_a: We don't participate in CDP scoring
Strategy.option_b: Our CDP score is D or D-
Strategy.option_c: Our CDP score is C or C-
Strategy.option_d: Our CDP score is B or B-
Strategy.option_e: Our CDP score is A or A-

Strategy.text: Do you have a science-based net-zero (or similar) target for your organisation?
Strategy.option_b: We are working on establishing science-based targets
Strategy.option_c: We are in the process of developing a Sustainability target, which is not quite net-zero yet
Strategy.option_d: Yes, we have net-zero target, but it's not science-based
Strategy.option_e: Yes, we have a science-based net-zero target that has been reviewed by an external party

Strategy.text: Do you have a defined roadmap to reach your net-zero target?
Strategy.option_b: We are creating a net-zero roadmap this year
Strategy.option_c: Yes, we have a high level net-zero roadmap that is being reviewed and resourced
Strategy.option_d: Yes, we have a detailed roadmap to reach our net-zero target that is sufficiently defined to be able to see the steps to reach net-zero

Strategy.text: How do you manage execution of your net-zero roadmap?
Strategy.option_b: We are in the process of agreeing the roadmap and its execution approach
Strategy.option_c: Our Sustainability manager is responsible for execution of the roadmap
Strategy.option_d: We have a small Sustainability team that is responsible for execution of our roadmap
Strategy.option_e: We have a Sustainability team in place supported by established programme management principles to execute our net-zero roadmap

Strategy.text: How are your net-zero targets and roadmap milestones reflected throughout your organisation?
Strategy.option_a: They are not or we don't have the targets
Strategy.option_b: We are working on translating our net-zero target into targets for all our departments
Strategy.option_c: Most of our leadership team have some net-zero related targets
Strategy.option_d: All leadership team and most of our managers have some net-zero related targets
Strategy.option_e: Each of our managers and departments across the whole organisation have a relevant portion of the overall net-zero target in their agenda to deliver

Strategy.text: How is environmental impact reflected in your IT/digital services strategy?
Strategy.option_b: We are currently considering how to reflect environmental impact in our IT/digital services strategy
Strategy.option_c: Our environmental impact is mentioned at a high level in our IT/digital services strategy
Strategy.option_d: Our IT/digital services strategy includes a specific section about our environmental impact and it is one of the core elements of our strategy

Strategy.text: Do you have a net-zero (or similar) target for your IT & digital services?
Strategy.option_b: We are working on defining a target for climate-related issues
Strategy.option_c: We have a climate-related target for the business, but not for IT services
Strategy.option_d: We have a target for our IT/digital services
Strategy.option_e: Yes, we have a science-based target for IT/digital services

Strategy.text: How is Sustainability reflected in your IT governance structure?
Strategy.option_a: Our IT governance structure does not include Sustainability
Strategy.option_b: We are working on making Sustainability a more explicit part of our IT governance
Strategy.option_c: We have a Sustainability representative in our organisation
Strategy.option_d: We are reviewing our IT governance structure to include Sustainability in it
Strategy.option_e: Sustainability issues have a firm and clear place in our IT governance structure

Strategy.text: Who is responsible for executing the Sustainability strategy of your IT/digital services?
Strategy.option_a: We don't have a Sustainability strategy covering IT/digital services
Strategy.option_b: Our leadership team has the responsibility, but they have no dedicated team
Strategy.option_c: We are creating & resourcing a team to ensure our Sustainability strategy is well executed, this team will report to senior leaders
Strategy.option_d: We have a dedicated team, but they don't have aligned budget as yet
Strategy.option_e: We have a dedicated team with a defined budget. They are responsible for execution, timelines, budgets, KPIs and targets

Strategy.text: How do you monitor the execution of your Sustainability roadmap in IT/digital services?
Strategy.option_b: We publish roadmap updates from time to time
Strategy.option_c: We have a set of metrics to track execution of our Sustainability roadmap
Strategy.option_d: We have a person who gathers metrics once per year/six months and prepares a report for the leadership team
Strategy.option_e: We have a Sustainability programme office that monitors execution and reports on all metrics every quarter

Strategy.text: Are there Sustainability targets included in the performance/bonus contracts of the senior IT leadership team?
Strategy.option_a: No, there are no specific targets
Strategy.option_b: Yes, I think some of the leadership team have targets included in their bonus plans
Strategy.option_c: Yes, our IT director has a Sustainability target included in their bonus plan and performance contract
Strategy.option_d: Yes, all/most of our leadership have Sustainability targets in their bonus plans and performance contracts
Strategy.option_e: Yes, all members of our senior IT leadership team have SMART (or similar) Sustainability targets included in their bonus plans & performance contracts

Strategy.text: Do you have a Sustainability manager role/team in your IT organisation?
Strategy.option_a: No, we don't have anyone like that
Strategy.option_b: We have a Sustainability representative in IT, but they are relatively junior
Strategy.option_c: Yes, we have appointed a Sustainability officer/manager but they sit outside of IT
Strategy.option_d: Yes, we have a Sustainability manager who is building their team
Strategy.option_e: Yes, we have a Sustainability team in IT/digital services who own our Sustainability strategy and roadmap

Strategy.text: How much visibility into GHG emissions do your CIO/CTO and their teams receive?
Strategy.option_b: They receive an annual compliance report
Strategy.option_c: They receive regular high level quarterly updates
Strategy.option_d: They get regular detailed monthly/quarterly reports and have access to more details if they require
Strategy.option_e: GHG emissions are one of their key metrics and they receive detailed update on a monthly basis split into scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions

Strategy.text: Do you have a defined and agreed budget for Sustainability in IT/digital services?
Strategy.option_a: No, we don't have any budget
Strategy.option_b: We assign budget for Sustainability projects on ad hoc basis
Strategy.option_c: We have a budget, but it is not sufficient
Strategy.option_d: Yes, we have a budget for all Sustainability initiatives in IT, but it requires more rigour in understanding how it is spent
Strategy.option_e: Yes, we have a defined and agreed budget for the execution of our Sustainability roadmap in IT, which is aligned with targets and monitored

Strategy.text: Do you require your IT suppliers to provide you with their Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions and their calculations?
Strategy.option_b: We don't right now but we're working on defining the right policy to require our suppliers to share their emissions with us
Strategy.option_c: Yes, we require our suppliers to send us their Scope 1 and 2 emissions
Strategy.option_d: Yes, we require our suppliers to share with us their Scope 1 and 2 emissions and we are expanding it to include Scope 3 in the (near) future
Strategy.option_e: Yes, we require all our IT suppliers to provide us with as detailed emission calculations as possible, including Scope 3

Strategy.text: Do you monitor your IT vendors/suppliers Sustainability performance?
Strategy.option_b: Not right now but we're planning to monitor it in the (near) future
Strategy.option_c: Yes, we ask for their Sustainability performance data when they are onboarded
Strategy.option_d: Yes, our suppliers provide Sustainability performance data when onboarded and during any subsequent RFP/tender process
Strategy.option_e: Yes, our suppliers provide us with their Sustainability performance once a year, we review it with them and expect them to make progress

Strategy.text: Do you have an inventory for environmental impact that includes GHG emissions?
Strategy.option_b: We have a few local databases that include the environmental impact information
Strategy.option_c: Yes, we have a central database with some environmental impact information
Strategy.option_d: Yes, we have an environmental impact inventory, which is updated manually once a year
Strategy.option_e: Yes, we have a dedicated inventory that is automatically updated with the most up-to-date environmental impact information

Strategy.text: How detailed and extensive is your Scope 3 emissions calculation?
Strategy.option_b: We calculate them at high level, using high level assumptions and emissions factors
Strategy.option_c: We have tried to calculate Scope 3 of some of our products' supply chain and we're planning to do more
Strategy.option_d: We have identified the most relevant Scope 3 emissions and calculate them for our supply chain
Strategy.option_e: We hire an external party to conduct a full life-cycle assessment of most of our products and services, including Scope 3 emissions

Strategy.text: What communication and engagement activities do you do around Sustainability across IT?
Strategy.option_b: We tend to include an aspect related to Sustainability in our regular IT events and updates
Strategy.option_c: We share updates on Sustainability and are planning to expand our agenda of Sustainability related events
Strategy.option_d: We organise several Sustainability events per year depending on the need and always include Sustainability in internal IT communication
Strategy.option_e: We have a fairly detailed plan of various Sustainability events (meetings, town-halls, etc) at different levels of the organisation

Governance & Management.text: Who owns Sustainability governance for IT (EUS, cloud, hardware, software, procurement)?
Governance & Management.option_d: Formal role or committee within IT accountable for Sustainability
Governance & Management.option_e: Dedicated IT Sustainability governance board with CxO oversight, aligned to enterprise ESG strategy

Governance & Management.text: How is accountability structured across IT and business functions?
Governance & Management.option_c: Partial alignment between IT, procurement, and ESG
Governance & Management.option_d: Cross-functional working group with defined roles
Governance & Management.option_e: RACI structure with clear accountability, escalation paths, and board-level reporting

Governance & Management.text: Do you have formal Sustainability policies or standards for IT?
Governance & Management.option_d: Documented IT Sustainability standards (refresh, procurement, development)
Governance & Management.option_e: Comprehensive IT Sustainability policy aligned with ISO/GBS/EPEAT/CSRD, audited regularly

Governance & Management.text: How do you manage Sustainability budgeting in IT?
Governance & Management.option_c: Some Sustainability cost estimates in IT budgets
Governance & Management.option_d: Dedicated line items for Sustainability projects
Governance & Management.option_e: Integrated multi-year budgets across IT, with ROI and cost–emissions forecasting

Governance & Management.text: Do you forecast IT Sustainability impacts (cost, energy, emissions)?
Governance & Management.option_d: Regular forecasts combining cost and emissions
Governance & Management.option_e: Integrated into TBM/FinOps/GreenOps with scenario analysis (refresh vs refurb, supplier A vs B)

Governance & Management.text: Do you benchmark IT Sustainability performance?
Governance & Management.option_d: Regular benchmarking against industry frameworks (EU CoC, SBTi, Gartner)
Governance & Management.option_e: Continuous benchmarking, results disclosed externally (e.g. CDP, ESG reports)

Governance & Management.text: How are best practices and frameworks adopted?
Governance & Management.option_d: Broad adoption (ISO 30134, UK GBS, CSRD, EU CoC)
Governance & Management.option_e: Fully embedded in standards, procurement, contracts, and reporting

Governance & Management.text: How are risks of inaction on IT Sustainability assessed?
Governance & Management.option_c: Some risks tracked in IT registers
Governance & Management.option_d: Formal Sustainability risks included in enterprise risk management
Governance & Management.option_e: Full IT risk register includes Sustainability, linked to enterprise compliance frameworks

Governance & Management.text: How do you ensure continuous improvement?
Governance & Management.option_c: Annual reviews of policies and performance
Governance & Management.option_d: Continuous improvement programme with KPIs and feedback loops
Governance & Management.option_e: Embedded culture of improvement with independent audits and annual targets

Governance & Management.text: How is Sustainability performance reported?
Governance & Management.option_d: Quarterly KPI reports shared across IT, ESG, and leadership
Governance & Management.option_e: Continuous dashboards feeding board/executive reports, disclosed externally

Governance & Management.text: How aligned is IT Sustainability governance with corporate climate goals (Net Zero, SBTi, CSR)?
Governance & Management.option_d: Aligned with corporate targets at high level
Governance & Management.option_e: Fully embedded — IT roadmaps directly support corporate climate/SBTi pathways

Governance & Management.text: Are Sustainability KPIs and incentives tied to individual roles?
Governance & Management.option_d: KPIs cascaded into IT leadership roles
Governance & Management.option_e: KPIs cascade to IT, procurement, architecture, with incentives/bonuses tied to performance

Governance & Management.text: Do you run change management and training for GreenOps adoption?
Governance & Management.option_d: Structured training and adoption programmes for IT teams
Governance & Management.option_e: Formal change management plan with mandatory training, user engagement, and behavioural nudges

Governance & Management.text: Who governs Sustainability data quality and assurance?
Governance & Management.option_d: Clear data ownership + internal audit/QA
Governance & Management.option_e: Independent assurance, confidence levels disclosed, third-party verification (e.g. limited/reasonable assurance)

Governance & Management.text: Do Sustainability criteria act as decision gates in IT governance?
Governance & Management.option_d: Mandatory stage gate for major IT projects
Governance & Management.option_e: Can block or reshape projects; Sustainability impact assessed alongside cost/security/performance

Governance & Management.text: How do you engage externally on IT Sustainability governance?
Governance & Management.option_d: Active in industry standards bodies (e.g. EU CoC, TBM/FinOps/GreenOps groups)
Governance & Management.option_e: Lead role in industry consortia, influencing regulations and shaping supplier behaviour

Governance & Management.text: Is Sustainability considered in IT programme/portfolio planning?
Governance & Management.option_b: Mentioned at portfolio level but no impact on decisions
Governance & Management.option_d: Regularly factored into portfolio planning alongside cost and risk
Governance & Management.option_e: Mandatory criterion in programme prioritisation, with quantified cost–emissions trade-offs

Governance & Management.text: Do architecture boards apply Sustainability stage gates?
Governance & Management.option_d: Consistently used as stage gates in reviews
Governance & Management.option_e: Mandatory Sustainability gate in all architecture/governance boards

Governance & Management.text: Are design principles for IT architecture sustainability-aware?
Governance & Management.option_c: Draft principles exist but inconsistently applied
Governance & Management.option_d: Formal architecture principles include energy efficiency, circularity, carbon impact
Governance & Management.option_e: Sustainability principles fully embedded, aligned to enterprise/industry standards (e.g. ISO 30134, EU CoC)

Governance & Management.text: Are trade-offs between performance, cost, and emissions assessed in design?
Governance & Management.option_d: Quantified trade-offs included in all solution designs
Governance & Management.option_e: Trade-offs mandatory, with executive-level approval for high-emission choices

Governance & Management.text: How is Sustainability risk tracked in project/programme delivery?
Governance & Management.option_d: Standard risk tracking in all projects
Governance & Management.option_e: Linked to enterprise risk management and board reporting

Financial & Service Management.text: Do you have a defined IT cost allocation model (services, portfolios, business units, environments)?
Financial & Service Management.option_a: No allocation model; IT spend tracked only at aggregate level.
Financial & Service Management.option_b: Partial allocation exists by department or tower, but inconsistent.
Financial & Service Management.option_c: Service-based costing is defined and applied across much of IT. Allocations are visible but not used systematically.
Financial & Service Management.option_d: Allocation is granular across services, sourcing models, and environments, reconciled with finance.
Financial & Service Management.option_e: Allocation is workload- and BU-specific, reconciled with sustainability metrics, and independently validated.

Financial & Service Management.text: How consistently are allocation methods applied across the enterprise?
Financial & Service Management.option_a: No consistency; each team reports differently.
Financial & Service Management.option_b: Some allocation rules exist but are applied unevenly.
Financial & Service Management.option_c: Defined standards exist and are applied in most areas, though exceptions remain.
Financial & Service Management.option_d: Allocation rules are enforced enterprise-wide with regular review.
Financial & Service Management.option_e: Methods are benchmarked externally and recognised as best practice.

Financial & Service Management.text: How are budgets allocated across IT services, portfolios, and sourcing models?
Financial & Service Management.option_a: Budgets are set at aggregate IT level.
Financial & Service Management.option_b: Budgets are partially broken down by department or tower.
Financial & Service Management.option_c: Budgets are allocated to services/portfolios but rarely tied to demand or sustainability.
Financial & Service Management.option_d: Budgets are granular and linked to demand, sourcing choices, and sustainability priorities.
Financial & Service Management.option_e: Budgets are dynamic, scenario-based, and directly linked to service sustainability outcomes.

Financial & Service Management.text: How are budgets reforecast to reflect demand or sourcing changes?
Financial & Service Management.option_b: Reforecasting occurs ad hoc when major variances arise.
Financial & Service Management.option_c: Reforecasting is scheduled but focuses only on cost.
Financial & Service Management.option_d: Reforecasting is automated and considers sourcing and demand shifts.
Financial & Service Management.option_e: Forecasting integrates financial and sustainability scenarios dynamically, informing governance decisions.

Financial & Service Management.text: How are forecasts created and reviewed?
Financial & Service Management.option_b: Forecasts use run-rates, with low accuracy.
Financial & Service Management.option_c: Forecasting is systematic, reviewed quarterly, with improving accuracy.
Financial & Service Management.option_d: Forecasts are automated, scenario-based, and reconciled with portfolio and sourcing strategies.
Financial & Service Management.option_e: Forecasts integrate carbon, energy, and cost; results are used at board level for strategic decision-making.

Financial & Service Management.text: To what extent do forecasts include scenario modelling for sustainability?
Financial & Service Management.option_b: Informal scenarios are run occasionally, with limited influence.
Financial & Service Management.option_c: Sustainability scenarios are included in some forecasts.
Financial & Service Management.option_d: Forecasting routinely incorporates scenarios such as workload placement, sourcing, or refresh cycles.
Financial & Service Management.option_e: Sustainability scenarios are automated and embedded in portfolio and sourcing strategy.

Financial & Service Management.text: How are sustainability metrics integrated into financial models?
Financial & Service Management.option_b: Mentioned in reports but not embedded.
Financial & Service Management.option_c: Linked to selected cost centres (cloud, data centre).
Financial & Service Management.option_d: Fully reconciled with cost and performance data, reviewed in governance forums.
Financial & Service Management.option_e: Sustainability metrics are embedded in allocation, budgeting, and forecasting, externally validated.

Financial & Service Management.text: What is the frequency and granularity of cost and sustainability reporting?
Financial & Service Management.option_b: Reports produced quarterly, inconsistent in detail.
Financial & Service Management.option_c: Reports produced monthly, with breakdowns by service or tower.
Financial & Service Management.option_d: Reports automated and reconciled with finance; cover cost, performance, and sustainability at service level.
Financial & Service Management.option_e: Reports are near real-time, granular to workload, externally assured, and disclosed publicly.

Financial & Service Management.text: How are reports tailored for different stakeholders (CIO, CFO, ESG, BU leaders)?
Financial & Service Management.option_b: Some tailoring occurs but outputs are inconsistent.
Financial & Service Management.option_c: Reports are produced for multiple audiences, but formats vary.
Financial & Service Management.option_d: Reports are tailored to each stakeholder group, consistent and automated.
Financial & Service Management.option_e: Reports are integrated into decision workflows, informing both internal stakeholders and external disclosure.

Financial & Service Management.text: To what extent are reporting processes automated and reconciled across functions?
Financial & Service Management.option_c: Automated for core services, reconciled with finance but not ESG data.
Financial & Service Management.option_d: Enterprise-wide automation reconciles finance, ops, and sustainability data.
Financial & Service Management.option_e: Fully automated, independently validated, and used for regulatory and investor disclosure.

Financial & Service Management.text: How mature are your defined TBM/FinOps methods and practices?
Financial & Service Management.option_c: TBM/FinOps methods defined and applied across most services.
Financial & Service Management.option_d: Methods embedded enterprise-wide, with oversight and training.
Financial & Service Management.option_e: Practices benchmarked externally, continuously improved, and used to shape industry standards.

Financial & Service Management.text: How is financial and sustainability data used in governance decisions?
Financial & Service Management.option_b: Data presented in governance forums but not acted upon.
Financial & Service Management.option_c: Data influences some sourcing and demand decisions.
Financial & Service Management.option_d: Integrated cost and sustainability reports guide investment, demand, and sourcing.
Financial & Service Management.option_e: Governance is consistently driven by integrated models; organisation influences industry standards.

Financial & Service Management.text: How do IT, finance, procurement, and sustainability functions collaborate?
Financial & Service Management.option_c: Cross-functional working groups exist, though maturity varies.
Financial & Service Management.option_d: Governance forums review cost and sustainability data with cross-functional accountability.
Financial & Service Management.option_e: Collaboration is embedded, with shared KPIs and joint ownership of outcomes.

Financial & Service Management.text: Do governance forums include joint accountability for cost and sustainability outcomes?
Financial & Service Management.option_c: Partial accountability included in charters or performance reviews.
Financial & Service Management.option_d: Clear joint accountability across IT, finance, and ESG leaders.
Financial & Service Management.option_e: Shared KPIs cascade across functions; incentives aligned with financial and sustainability outcomes.

Financial & Service Management.text: How integrated are financial and sustainability systems and tooling?
Financial & Service Management.option_c: Partial integration between finance and IT tooling, but ESG data separate.
Financial & Service Management.option_d: Fully integrated across TBM/FinOps, finance, and ESG platforms.
Financial & Service Management.option_e: Systems provide a single source of truth, with advanced analytics and benchmarking.

Supply Chain.text: How is Sustainability weighted in procurement decisions?
Supply Chain.option_c: Considered at corporate level (supplier ESG ratings)
Supply Chain.option_d: Explicit weighting in tenders (10–20%), includes product-level criteria
Supply Chain.option_e: High weighting (>30%) with mandatory product-level LCA/PCF, circularity and emissions thresholds

Supply Chain.text: At what level do you assess supplier Sustainability?
Supply Chain.option_c: Corporate + some product eco-labels (e.g. EnergyStar, TCO)
Supply Chain.option_d: Product-level embodied carbon, recyclability, repairability considered
Supply Chain.option_e: End-to-end lifecycle data (scope 1–3, transport, packaging, take-back) with external validation

Supply Chain.text: How are procurement levers used to minimise environmental impact?
Supply Chain.option_d: Systematic levers applied (low-carbon logistics, renewable sourcing, refurbished optioning)
Supply Chain.option_e: All procurement levers optimised across categories, tracked and reported

Supply Chain.text: Do you engage suppliers on Sustainability?
Supply Chain.option_c: RFPs include Sustainability but with no follow-up
Supply Chain.option_e: Long-term partnerships with joint innovation, shared dashboards, co-investment in circularity

Supply Chain.text: How are internal stakeholders engaged on procurement Sustainability?
Supply Chain.option_c: Shared with IT/ESG teams for awareness
Supply Chain.option_d: Jointly defined criteria with IT, Finance, ESG
Supply Chain.option_e: Cross-functional governance board with authority to enforce Sustainability procurement strategy

Supply Chain.text: How do you validate supplier claims?
Supply Chain.option_d: Independent audits or LCA checks for high-impact categories
Supply Chain.option_e: Continuous monitoring + third-party validation embedded into contracts

Supply Chain.text: How do you handle transportation and logistics in procurement?
Supply Chain.option_c: Some consideration of mode (air vs sea)
Supply Chain.option_e: Transport emissions included in scoring, mandatory low/zero-carbon options where available

Supply Chain.text: Do you include circularity in procurement criteria?
Supply Chain.option_d: Circularity criteria (reuse, repair, ITAD integration) included in RFPs
Supply Chain.option_e: Circular-first sourcing mandated, with reuse/avoidance metrics tracked

Supply Chain.text: How do you manage ongoing supplier performance?
Supply Chain.option_d: Quarterly Sustainability KPIs included in reviews
Supply Chain.option_e: Live dashboards, penalties/rewards tied to supplier Sustainability outcomes

Supply Chain.text: How are product-level emissions factored into procurement?
Supply Chain.option_c: Embodied carbon considered in some categories
Supply Chain.option_d: Product-level LCA/PCF required for major categories
Supply Chain.option_e: Product-level LCA/PCF mandatory across all tenders, integrated into TBM/FinOps/GreenOps tooling

Supply Chain.text: Do Sustainability criteria influence actual contract awards?
Supply Chain.option_c: Sometimes used, but commercial factors dominate
Supply Chain.option_e: Sustainability is a decisive factor — suppliers rejected if they fail to meet thresholds

Supply Chain.option_c: Available as an option but not mandated
Supply Chain.option_d: Widely sourced where viable, supported by policy
Supply Chain.option_e: Refurb/renew is default, with new purchases justified by exception

Supply Chain.text: Do you reject or penalise poor Sustainability performance?
Supply Chain.option_d: Contracts include Sustainability SLAs and may influence renewal
Supply Chain.option_e: Poor performers are rejected/penalised, high performers rewarded via contract terms

Supply Chain.text: How is supplier data integrated into internal systems?
Supply Chain.option_c: Aggregated at corporate ESG level only
Supply Chain.option_d: Integrated into procurement dashboards, some link to ITAM/ESG reporting
Supply Chain.option_e: Fully integrated into TBM/FinOps/ITAM/GreenOps tooling for decision-making

Supply Chain.text: How aligned is procurement with regulations & standards (CSRD, EU taxonomy, UK GBS, EPEAT, etc.)?
Supply Chain.option_d: Policies formally align with CSRD/UK GBS/EPEAT for major categories
Supply Chain.option_e: Full regulatory compliance built in, preparing for future obligations with audits and roadmaps

Data Centre.text: Who owns the Sustainability agenda for your data centres?
Data Centre.option_e: The Sustainability agenda is owned by the data centre team with proactive alignment across all aspects of IT

Data Centre.text: Do you have a budget specifically committed to Sustainability initiatives within your data centre team?
Data Centre.option_b: Not really, although we do have a budget for energy efficiency
Data Centre.option_c: No, there is an overall budget, which includes miscellaneous items such as Sustainability
Data Centre.option_d: Yes, we do but it has not grown over the last 5 years
Data Centre.option_e: Yes, we do and it's been growing over the last 5 years

Data Centre.text: Does your overarching data centre strategy consider Sustainability?
Data Centre.option_b: It doesn't, but we're planning on including it in the near future
Data Centre.option_c: It's included in a very basic way
Data Centre.option_d: It's identified as a key component/design consideration, where basic metrics and recommendations are defined
Data Centre.option_e: Sustainability is a core element of our data centre strategy, which is fully aligned with the broader corporate Sustainability strategy

Data Centre.text: How does your data centre Sustainability strategy align with your wider IT & corporate Sustainability strategies?
Data Centre.option_a: We don't have a data centre Sustainability strategy
Data Centre.option_b: We have one but it doesn’t align with the wider IT and corporate Sustainability strategies
Data Centre.option_e: It's fully aligned, well mapped and documented

Data Centre.text: Do you regularly benchmark your data centre performance with regards to Sustainability?
Data Centre.option_a: No we don't, and we have no plans to do so in future
Data Centre.option_b: No, but we have plans to do so in the near future
Data Centre.option_c: We benchmark internally, across our data centre estate
Data Centre.option_d: We benchmark both internally and externally
Data Centre.option_e: We benchmark both internally and externally, with the output published and included in our CSR collateral

Data Centre.text: How compliant is your data centre with EPA Energy Star/ EU Eco Design/EU Code of Conduct or similar guidelines?
Data Centre.option_a: We don't follow a standard or set of guidelines
Data Centre.option_b: We use our own internal set of standards and guidelines
Data Centre.option_c: We're aware of the set of guidelines highlighted, but we don't follow them to the letter
Data Centre.option_d: We're aware and compliant with the guidelines mentioned, but we've not been certified
Data Centre.option_e: We're compliant and certified with the guidelines you've mentioned, and we follow our own internal set of standards and recommendations

Data Centre.text: Do you apply Sustainability principles to your operational decision-making within your data centre?
Data Centre.option_a: No, we don't and we have no plans to do so
Data Centre.option_b: No, we don't but we have plans to make decisions with Sustainability in mind in future
Data Centre.option_c: Yes we do, but only when the decision is entirely within our control
Data Centre.option_d: Yes we do and we communicate with different teams who may themselves have different operational priorities
Data Centre.option_e: Yes we do and all other teams are operating with aligned Sustainability priorities and principles

Data Centre.text: Do you review data centre resource usage to assess adherence to company carbon reduction targets & Sustainability policies?
Data Centre.option_a: No, and we don't have carbon reduction policies
Data Centre.option_b: No, however we do have carbon reduction policies
Data Centre.option_c: Yes, but rarely in conjunction with company carbon reduction policies
Data Centre.option_d: Yes, in conjunction with company carbon reduction policies annually
Data Centre.option_e: Yes, in conjunction with company carbon reduction policies monthly

Data Centre.text: How do you monitor energy consumption (Scope 2 emissions) of your data centre?
Data Centre.option_a: We don't record any energy consumption data
Data Centre.option_b: We record energy consumption data but don't use it for monitoring or detailed analysis
Data Centre.option_c: We manually analyse the energy invoices on a periodic basis (less than twice a year)
Data Centre.option_d: We feed energy consumption data into a repository which we use to create monthly or annual consumption reports
Data Centre.option_e: There is an automatic link between our systems and our energy supplier which provides us near real time monitoring

Data Centre.text: Do you regularly review data centre efficiency & power consumption from the perspective of Sustainability?
Data Centre.option_a: No, we rarely review operational performance goals
Data Centre.option_b: We review efficiency and power consumption regularly, but not from a sustainability perspective
Data Centre.option_c: Yes, we review this data from a Sustainability perspective annually
Data Centre.option_d: Yes, we review this data from a sustainability perspective monthly
Data Centre.option_e: Yes, we review this data from a sustainability perspective in near real time

Data Centre.text: How do you capture the Sustainability data of your data centre?
Data Centre.option_a: We use the energy supplier invoices to capture data centre sustainability metrics
Data Centre.option_b: We use building level data capture and store it in a repository
Data Centre.option_c: We use room level data capture and store it in a repository
Data Centre.option_d: We use rack level data capture and store it in a repository
Data Centre.option_e: We use rack level data capture and store it in a repository where it is reviewed and shared internally with stakeholders

Data Centre.text: How frequently do you update your GHG inventory with data centre Sustainability data?
Data Centre.option_a: We don't have a GHG emissions inventory
Data Centre.option_b: We have a GHG emissions inventory but don't input data from the data centre
Data Centre.option_e: We update it automatically at least daily

Data Centre.text: How frequently do you calculate your GHG emissions?
Data Centre.option_a: We don't and we have no plans to
Data Centre.option_b: We don't today but we have plans to start
Data Centre.option_c: We calculate it annually but it doesn't play a role in decision making
Data Centre.option_d: We calculate it monthly or better, but it doesn't play a role in decision making
Data Centre.option_e: We automatically calculate it, and it plays a key role in decision making

Data Centre.text: How do you calculate Scope 3 emissions for your Data Centre(s)?
Data Centre.option_a: We haven't calculated our data centre scope 3 emissions as we do not have the information from our suppliers
Data Centre.option_b: We have calculated our data centre scope 3 emissions a few times in the past and it's too complicated and we dont have enough resources to do it on a consistent basis.
Data Centre.option_c: We have a central Sustainability team who's job it is to calculate scope 3 emissions for data centres
Data Centre.option_d: We calculate scope 3 emissions ourselves using aggregated and approximated information from national annexes
Data Centre.option_e: We calculate scope 3 emissions ourselves using information provided by suppliers/data collected by ourselves and have this externally validated

Data Centre.text: How often does the data centre team prepare and share Sustainability reports?
Data Centre.option_a: We don't prepare or share Sustainability reports for data centres
Data Centre.option_b: Data centre sustainability reports are prepared annually but rarely shared
Data Centre.option_c: Data centre sustainability reports are prepared annually and shared with the Sustainability team and/or higher management
Data Centre.option_d: Data centre sustainability reports are prepared monthly and shared with the Sustainability team and/or higher management
Data Centre.option_e: The data centre sustainability data is captured continuously and automatically fed into a Sustainability platform used by the Sustainability team/higher management

Data Centre.text: Has your data centre team undertaken training related to the Sustainability of data centre facilities & operations?
Data Centre.option_a: No, we don't have or offer sustainability training for the data centre or operations teams
Data Centre.option_b: Not yet, but we are planning to offer training in the future (assuming we have budget)
Data Centre.option_c: Yes, we train our staff on general Sustainability
Data Centre.option_d: Yes, we occasionally run training exercises for our staff on IT specific Sustainability best practises
Data Centre.option_e: Yes, we regularly train our staff IT specific Sustainability best practise

Data Centre.text: Do you share Data Centre Sustainability performance with your teams responsible for running hardware inside of racks (servers, storage, networking etc.)?
Data Centre.option_a: We don't track or share sustainability performance
Data Centre.option_b: We track sustainability performance but don't share it with other teams
Data Centre.option_c: We track sustainability performance and share it manually on an ad-hoc basis, when it's requested.
Data Centre.option_d: We track sustainability performance and share it on an anual basis, such as an annual review
Data Centre.option_e: We track and share this information regularly (and automatically) with the correct teams to optimise their decision making

Core Infrastructure.text: Who owns the Sustainability agenda within your hardware Infrastructure team?
Core Infrastructure.option_a: We don't have a formal sustainability agenda in the team
Core Infrastructure.option_b: Higher management own the sustainability agenda
Core Infrastructure.option_c: The Sustainability team own the Sustainability agenda and liaise with higher management/data centre operations teams
Core Infrastructure.option_d: The Infrastructure team own the sustainability agenda
Core Infrastructure.option_e: The sustainability agenda is split across multiple teams (Sustainability, higher management, Hardware Infrastructure)

Core Infrastructure.text: Do you have Sustainability principles to guide your operational and tactical decision-making?
Core Infrastructure.option_a: No, we don't and we currently have no plans for sustainability to guide our decision making process currently
Core Infrastructure.option_b: No we don't but we do have plans to make decisions with Sustainability in mind
Core Infrastructure.option_c: Yes we do apply Sustainability principles in operational decision making, but only when the decision is entirely within our control
Core Infrastructure.option_d: Yes we do apply Sustainability principles in operational decision making, and we communicate with different teams who may themselves have different operational goals/priorities
Core Infrastructure.option_e: Yes we do apply Sustainability principles in operational decision making, and all other teams are operating with aligned Sustainability priorities and principles

Core Infrastructure.text: Do you have a budget specifically committed to Sustainability initiatives?
Core Infrastructure.option_a: No we don't have a platform budget specifically committed to sustainability
Core Infrastructure.option_b: Not really, though we have a budget for energy efficiency
Core Infrastructure.option_c: No, there is an overall budget, which includes miscellaneous items such as Sustainability (but this is not specifically allocated)
Core Infrastructure.option_d: Yes we do have a Sustainability budget, but it has not grown over the last 5 years
Core Infrastructure.option_e: Yes we do have a Sustainability budget, and it's been growing every year as our focus increases

Core Infrastructure.text: How are hardware infrastructure decisions aligned with wider IT Sustainability goals?
Core Infrastructure.option_a: We take a performance first approach, Sustainability doesn't enter into our decision making
Core Infrastructure.option_b: We take a performance first approach, retrospectively we then consider sustainability
Core Infrastructure.option_c: We take a performance first approach, with Sustainability being an important secondary criteria
Core Infrastructure.option_d: Sustainability is an important factor in team decision making, and our decisions align with Sustainability Goals
Core Infrastructure.option_e: Sustainability is a core non-functional design criteria, and we've created an intensity metric to track improvements in sustainability over time. This aligns with wider Sustainability Goals

Core Infrastructure.text: Do you regularly review physical hardware usage to assess adherence to company carbon reduction targets & policies?
Core Infrastructure.option_a: No, hardware usage is not reviewed from the context of company CSR carbon reduction targets
Core Infrastructure.option_b: Yes, we track scope 1 & 2 emissions on an annual basis for the purpose of Sustainability reporting
Core Infrastructure.option_c: Hardware usage, including embodied energy from vendor provided Product Carbon Footprints, are assessed against company CSR policies and reported annually
Core Infrastructure.option_d: Hardware usage, including embodied energy and scope 1 and 2 emissions are assessed against company CSR policies and reported quarterly
Core Infrastructure.option_e: Yes, scope 1,2 & 3 metrics are tracked in our automated tooling at a granular level, reviewed monthly with a proven process for identification and remediation of waste with agreed KPIs and targets

Core Infrastructure.text: How compliant is your IT infrastructure with CENELEC / EU Eco design or similar guidelines?
Core Infrastructure.option_a: We've never heard of these guidelines (or don't use them)
Core Infrastructure.option_b: We assumed the hardware manufacturers and designers were already compliant when we procured this hardware
Core Infrastructure.option_c: We've ensured we're compliant with certain standards and frameworks, but couldn't specifically name them
Core Infrastructure.option_d: We're compliant with standards and frameworks including lot 9 amendments, Energy Star labelling program for IT equipment, EU Eco guidelines etc
Core Infrastructure.option_e: In addition to full compliance, we're able to determine the in-use energy efficiency of hardware through more advanced tools

Core Infrastructure.text: Do you actively benchmark your hardware with regards to Sustainability?
Core Infrastructure.option_a: No, we don't benchmark hardware with regard to sustainability
Core Infrastructure.option_b: We are planning to, but currently we don't benchmark our hardware with regard to Sustainability
Core Infrastructure.option_c: We have communicated several benchmarking studies with our team, it is up to them how this information is used
Core Infrastructure.option_d: We've benchmarked the performance of our hardware in the past and review when required
Core Infrastructure.option_e: Yes, we benchmark our hardware on a regular basis (including third party validation) and track this against KPIs and targets that are also regularly reviewed

Core Infrastructure.text: How do you monitor energy consumption of your physical hardware infrastructure?
Core Infrastructure.option_a: We don't monitor energy consumption of our hardware, just overall Data Centre
Core Infrastructure.option_b: We have energy consumption data at the rack level
Core Infrastructure.option_c: We have energy consumption data at the rack level and do adhoc measurements of energy consumption at server level
Core Infrastructure.option_d: We monitor energy consumption of all our IT equipment at server level plus across a few other components
Core Infrastructure.option_e: Everything is monitored in real time and the data is stored in a central repository, and visualized on a dashboard

Core Infrastructure.text: Do you review your hardware efficiency & energy consumption from the perspective of Sustainability?
Core Infrastructure.option_a: We don't review hardware efficiency or energy consumption from the perspective of sustainability
Core Infrastructure.option_b: We have started discussing tools that might help us monitor efficiency and energy consumption of our hardware but do not have them deployed (or budget to purchase)
Core Infrastructure.option_c: We have undertaken a few projects to find out the efficiency of our hardware, but this is not scheduled nor regularly reviewed
Core Infrastructure.option_d: We use some high level assumptions and data to consider energy consumption of operating our hardware. The calculations are based upon information provided by our vendors
Core Infrastructure.option_e: We use tools that monitor efficiency and energy consumption of our hardware in real time and we have targets for minimizing energy consumption

Core Infrastructure.text: How frequently do you update your Environmental Impact / GHG Inventory with physical hardware Sustainability data?
Core Infrastructure.option_a: We don't have such a GHG inventory for hardware or platforms
Core Infrastructure.option_b: We don't update the GHG inventory at the moment and/or don't have specific Sustainability data in the platform team
Core Infrastructure.option_c: We manually provide the Sustainability team with some data as required (on an adhoc basis)
Core Infrastructure.option_d: We have a KPI to provide annual updates for the Environmental Impact (or GHG) inventory
Core Infrastructure.option_e: We have established an automated connection to upload Sustainability relevant data from the platform team to the Environmental Impact (or GHG) inventory

Core Infrastructure.text: How aware are you of Scope 3 emissions of your hardware?
Core Infrastructure.option_a: We don't know what Scope 3 emissions are and do not monitor them (our vendors do not supply this data)
Core Infrastructure.option_b: We are aware of Scope 3 emissions, but don't know how to calculate them
Core Infrastructure.option_c: We have a description and basic methodology for calculating and understanding our Scope 3 emissions. This uses high level general estimates rather than device specific metrics
Core Infrastructure.option_d: We calculate our Scope 3 emissions using models with high level assumptions at a detailed level
Core Infrastructure.option_e: We calculate and report Scope 3 emissions of our activities using measured (real) data from our activities

Core Infrastructure.text: How often does your IT Infrastructure team prepare and share Sustainability reports?
Core Infrastructure.option_b: We do it only when requested
Core Infrastructure.option_c: We do it annually as part of the compliance process
Core Infrastructure.option_d: We have an automated monthly reporting system that shares within and outside of the team
Core Infrastructure.option_e: We have a sustainability dashboard that removes the need for regular report making. This is visible to and shared with multiple teams across the business and IT organisation

Core Infrastructure.text: Are the members of your hardware Infrastructure team trained how to minimise the environmental impact of hardware?
Core Infrastructure.option_a: No, they are not trained specifically to minimise the environmental impact of our hardware platforms
Core Infrastructure.option_b: They are free to use the tools that we have available to our platforms and operations personnel
Core Infrastructure.option_c: Yes, we have groups/communities that serve the purpose of training and both efficiency and Sustainability are part of it
Core Infrastructure.option_d: Yes, our employees receive some training how to operate hardware efficiently, with some basic standards and targets
Core Infrastructure.option_e: Yes, our team members are well trained to operate hardware efficiently, to minimise its impact on the environment. We have tools and processes in place to measure it, which we continuously use, track and report to the wider IT teams

Core Infrastructure.text: Do you share the Sustainability performance of hardware platforms with your Development teams?
Core Infrastructure.option_a: No, we don't track the sustainability performance of hardware or share it with other teams
Core Infrastructure.option_b: We track Sustainability performance of hardware, but don't share it with the development teams
Core Infrastructure.option_c: Hardware and platform sustainability performance is considered and shared in the design phase only
Core Infrastructure.option_d: The software team receives regular manual updates on the Sustainability performance of the hardware platforms
Core Infrastructure.option_e: The software team receives the sustainability profile and regular reports on efficiency and utilisation and uses it in their development and testing process. This process is automated and is delivered in as near to real-time as can be achieved

AI & Advanced Compute.text: How are AI workload emissions measured?
AI & Advanced Compute.option_d: Node- or job-level tracking of energy/emissions
AI & Advanced Compute.option_e: Full lifecycle tracking (training + inference) at workload level, including embodied + operational emissions

AI & Advanced Compute.text: Do you consider water and cooling impacts of AI workloads?
AI & Advanced Compute.option_e: Tracked in real time, optimised across workload placement

AI & Advanced Compute.text: How is AI model training justified in business cases?
AI & Advanced Compute.option_d: Training requires cost + performance + sustainability assessment
AI & Advanced Compute.option_e: Executive approval mandatory, with quantified cost–carbon trade-offs

AI & Advanced Compute.text: Do you optimise AI models for efficiency (size, reuse, inference)?
AI & Advanced Compute.option_d: Standard practice: small models, distillation, transfer learning, reuse prioritised
AI & Advanced Compute.option_e: Efficiency-first strategy embedded: models selected/optimised to minimise emissions across lifecycle

AI & Advanced Compute.text: How do you manage AI hardware lifecycle (GPUs/TPUs)?
AI & Advanced Compute.option_d: Monitor embodied + operational emissions at hardware level
AI & Advanced Compute.option_e: Full lifecycle PCF/LCA of GPUs/TPUs, linked to ITAM/GreenOps, with reuse/resale optimisation

AI & Advanced Compute.text: How do you decide placement of AI workloads (cloud, private, HPC)?
AI & Advanced Compute.option_d: Sustainability included in workload placement decisions
AI & Advanced Compute.option_e: “Right place, right time, right platform” applied: workload placement optimised for cost, carbon, water, and security

AI & Advanced Compute.text: How aligned is AI Sustainability with AI ethics/governance?
AI & Advanced Compute.option_d: Formal alignment between ethics, risk, and Sustainability governance
AI & Advanced Compute.option_e: Unified AI governance covering ethics, risk, and Sustainability

AI & Advanced Compute.text: Do you integrate AI Sustainability data into FinOps/TBM/GreenOps dashboards?
AI & Advanced Compute.option_c: Some AI data included in reporting
AI & Advanced Compute.option_d: AI data integrated into GreenOps and FinOps
AI & Advanced Compute.option_e: Fully integrated, real-time AI Sustainability metrics in TBM/FinOps/GreenOps, used for decision-making

AI & Advanced Compute.text: How do you manage AI workload lifecycle (data prep → training → deployment → retirement)?
AI & Advanced Compute.option_e: Full AI lifecycle managed with Sustainability controls from data prep to model retirement

Cloud Ops.text: Who owns the Sustainability agenda within your cloud operations team?
Cloud Ops.option_a: We don't have a specific Sustainability agenda in the team, we assume that is the vendor's responsibility
Cloud Ops.option_c: The Sustainability team own the agenda and liaise with senior management & the cloud operations teams
Cloud Ops.option_d: The cloud team owns the agenda
Cloud Ops.option_e: The agenda is split across multiple teams (Sustainability, senior management & cloud operations)

Cloud Ops.text: How are your cloud decisions aligned with wider IT efficiency and Sustainability goals?
Cloud Ops.option_a: We take a performance first approach, Sustainability doesn't enter into our decision making
Cloud Ops.option_b: We take a performance first approach. Retrospectively we do then consider sustainability, but it is not mandatory
Cloud Ops.option_c: We take a performance first approach, Sustainability is a secondary criteria
Cloud Ops.option_d: Sustainability is an important factor in team decision making, and our decisions often align with Sustainability goals
Cloud Ops.option_e: Sustainability is a critical factor in decision making. Standard processes ensure alignment with Sustainability goals

Cloud Ops.text: Do you have a FinOps team/resource that is focused on optimising and reporting on your cloud usage, including implementing cost savings initiatives?
Cloud Ops.option_a: No, we don't have a FinOps team
Cloud Ops.option_b: We have someone who periodically looks at cloud usage but it is not a defined role
Cloud Ops.option_c: Yes, we have someone allocated and have identified some savings
Cloud Ops.option_d: Yes, we have a structured FinOps process and have implemented some savings but do not have defined KPIs or targets
Cloud Ops.option_e: Yes, our FinOps function is very mature and embedded into the wider organisation. We regularly report on cloud usage trends and have defined KPIs and targets

Cloud Ops.text: Do you have a budget specifically committed to Sustainability initiatives?
Cloud Ops.option_a: No we don't have a specific Sustainability budget inside the team
Cloud Ops.option_b: Not really, though we have basic budget allocated for FinOps tooling to optimise usage
Cloud Ops.option_c: No, there is an overall budget, which includes miscellaneous items such as Sustainability
Cloud Ops.option_d: Yes we do have a Sustainability budget, but it has not grown over the last 5 years
Cloud Ops.option_e: Yes we do have a Sustainability budget, and it's grown year on year

Cloud Ops.text: Do you regularly review cloud usage to assess adherence to company carbon reduction targets & policies?
Cloud Ops.option_a: No, cloud usage is not reviewed from the context of carbon reduction targets
Cloud Ops.option_b: Yes, but only at a basic level on an annual basis using vendor provided metrics at an account level
Cloud Ops.option_c: Yes, we track usage on a quarterly basis using vendor provided emissions metrics at an account level
Cloud Ops.option_d: Yes, usage and estimated emissions are tracked at a granular level and reviewed monthly with a team/resource focussed on identifying waste
Cloud Ops.option_e: Yes, usage and calculated scope 1, 2 & 3 metrics are tracked in our automated tooling at a granular level, reviewed monthly with a proven process for identification and remediation of waste with agreed KPIs and targets

Cloud Ops.text: Do you actively benchmark your cloud providers with regards to Sustainability?
Cloud Ops.option_a: No, we do not benchmark our providers
Cloud Ops.option_b: Yes, we manually review their available information on an annual basis
Cloud Ops.option_c: Yes, we manually review their available information on an bi-annual basis
Cloud Ops.option_d: Yes, we review internally and validate with external 3rd parties on a quarterly basis, including tracking their adherence with their carbon reduction programme
Cloud Ops.option_e: Yes, we review internally and validate with external 3rd parties on a monthly basis, including tracking their adherence with their carbon reduction programme

Cloud Ops.text: How do you monitor GHG emissions and energy consumption of your cloud services?
Cloud Ops.option_a: We do not track energy consumption or carbon emissions for cloud services
Cloud Ops.option_b: We are unable to accurately track energy consumption as the data is not made available but use the basic information provided by our cloud vendor for carbon emissions
Cloud Ops.option_c: We manually gather vendor provided metrics for carbon emissions on a monthly basis and have our own estimates for energy consumption
Cloud Ops.option_d: We use vendor tooling that tracks scope 1 & 2 carbon emissions from our cloud vendors in near real time
Cloud Ops.option_e: We use externally validated tooling that tracks scope 1, 2 & 3 emissions from our cloud vendors in near real time

Cloud Ops.text: How do you calculate and track the GHG emissions of your cloud (Scope 3)?
Cloud Ops.option_a: We do not track Scope 3 emissions from our cloud providers
Cloud Ops.option_b: We use estimates from the provider
Cloud Ops.option_c: We use the vendor provided tooling to track Scope 3 estimates at an account level on a quarterly basis
Cloud Ops.option_d: We use vendor provided and/or independent tooling to proactively track Scope 3 emissions on a monthly basis at an account level
Cloud Ops.option_e: We use vendor provided and/or independent tooling to proactively track Scope 3 emissions and feed this into our FinOps team with agreed KPIs and targets

Cloud Ops.text: How frequently do you calculate the avoided GHG emissions of your cloud services?
Cloud Ops.option_a: We do not calculate avoided GHG emissions
Cloud Ops.option_d: Monthly - with a manual process that is externally validated
Cloud Ops.option_e: In near real time, via an automated process and externally validated calculations

Cloud Ops.text: How frequently do you update your environmental impact & GHG inventory with cloud Sustainability data?
Cloud Ops.option_a: We do not update our GHG inventory with cloud data
Cloud Ops.option_b: Once a year, with manually gathered estimates
Cloud Ops.option_c: Once every 6-months, with manually gathered estimates
Cloud Ops.option_d: Once a quarter, using vendor metrics
Cloud Ops.option_e: Every month, it's an automated process

Cloud Ops.text: How often does your cloud team prepare and share Sustainability reports?
Cloud Ops.option_a: We don't prepare or share Sustainability reports
Cloud Ops.option_b: Reports are prepared annually but rarely shared
Cloud Ops.option_c: Reports are prepared annually and shared with the Sustainability team & senior management
Cloud Ops.option_d: Reports are prepared monthly and shared with the Sustainability team & senior management
Cloud Ops.option_e: The data is captured continuously and automatically fed into a Sustainability platform used by the Sustainability team & senior management

Cloud Ops.text: Do you review how your cloud providers are operating from a Sustainability perspective?
Cloud Ops.option_b: Yes, we use the information publicly available on their website when choosing vendors
Cloud Ops.option_c: Yes, we review the publicly available information and independent analyst reports on an annual basis
Cloud Ops.option_d: Yes, we review public information and require our vendors to present updates in our quarterly account review sessions
Cloud Ops.option_e: Yes, we use external independent analysts to review information provided by our vendors, assessing their adherence with their agreed targets

Cloud Ops.text: Is your cloud operations team(s) trained in the area of Sustainability?
Cloud Ops.option_a: No, they have not had any specific Sustainability related training
Cloud Ops.option_b: They are free to use the basic training tools and resources that we have available
Cloud Ops.option_c: Yes, we have groups & communities that serve the purpose of training and efficiency - Sustainability is part of it
Cloud Ops.option_d: Yes, our team members receive some standardised training how to design and operate cloud resources efficiently from the context of Sustainability
Cloud Ops.option_e: Yes, our team(s) is well trained to operate cloud efficiently and to minimise its impact on the environment. We have tools to measure efficiency, which are continuously used

Cloud Ops.text: Do your software development teams share projected workload characteristics with you when deploying into the Cloud?
Cloud Ops.option_a: No, workload profiles are not shared, resources are built and deployed as documented
Cloud Ops.option_b: We have limited access but tend to build as per standards
Cloud Ops.option_c: We have done in the past and planning to do it more often in the future
Cloud Ops.option_d: Yes, from time to time we discuss how to best optimise workloads and cloud resources
Cloud Ops.option_e: Yes, we regularly meet with our developers to understand how to optimise code development with the cloud resources

Software Development.text: Who owns the Sustainability agenda within your software development team(s)?
Software Development.option_a: No one currently owns the Sustainability agenda within our development teams
Software Development.option_b: We have discussed appointing an owner for Sustainability and we're planning to do this in the near future
Software Development.option_c: We have a contact who is liaising with the IT & business Sustainability manager
Software Development.option_d: The Sustainability agenda is owned by one of our team members who promotes it within the team and works with the business Sustainability manager
Software Development.option_e: We have a dedicated role at the manager level that owns the Sustainability agenda for the software development function

Software Development.text: Do you have a budget for Sustainability initiatives within your development team?

Software Development.option_b: We rely on the central Sustainability team to provide us with some financial support to execute projects
Software Development.option_c: We agree budgets on a project by project basis. This includes Sustainability objectives
Software Development.option_d: Yes, we have a (relatively) small, dedicated budget to deliver a few Sustainability projects during the year
Software Development.option_e: Yes, we have a dedicated, annual budget for our Sustainability agenda within the Software Development team

Software Development.text: Does your overarching strategy for software development consider Sustainability?
Software Development.option_a: Our software development strategy doesn't include Sustainability today (or we don't have Software Design & Development Strategy)
Software Development.option_b: We are planning to include environmental impact in our software design & development strategy
Software Development.option_c: Sustainability is included at a basic high level as part of our software design & development strategy, but is not mandatory
Software Development.option_d: Sustainability is identified as a key component & design consideration, with basic metrics and recommendations defined
Software Development.option_e: Sustainability is a core element of our software design & development strategy, which is fully aligned with the business Sustainability strategy & CSR

Software Development.text: Do you have environmental impact principles guiding your design and development processes?
Software Development.option_a: No, we don't (yet) have environmental impact principles guiding our development processes
Software Development.option_b: We discuss environmental impact in our development teams, but no formal principles or automated processes
Software Development.option_c: We are working on creating a set of environmental impact principles for our development processes
Software Development.option_d: Yes, we have a set of standard defined principles. Processes are in place to ensure adherence, but these are not measured
Software Development.option_e: Yes, we do have a set of formal Sustainability principles for development that our teams are measured against

Software Development.text: How do you know if your Sustainability principles make a real difference?
Software Development.option_a: We don't know if our principles are making a tangible impact
Software Development.option_b: We plan on including a set of Sustainability principles in our reviews and KPI/target setting processes but do not measure impact today
Software Development.option_c: We have discussions with our teams to understand the value of the principles and manually assess their impact
Software Development.option_d: As part of our development process, we regularly but manually evaluate the effectiveness of the principles and track the impact against basic Sustainability targets
Software Development.option_e: As part of our development process, we regularly evaluate execution against our Sustainability objectives, with progress tracked against defined targets and KPIs

Software Development.text: How do you monitor energy consumption (scope 2 emissions) of your software?
Software Development.option_a: We don't monitor or track energy consumption related to software usage
Software Development.option_b: We have started discussing about some tools that can help us monitor energy consumption of our software but have nothing (or limited) capability to do this in place today
Software Development.option_c: We have undertaken several projects to find out how much energy our applications consume
Software Development.option_d: We use some high level assumptions and data to consider energy consumption of our software
Software Development.option_e: We use automated tooling that monitors energy consumption of our software in real time and we have targets for minimising energy consumption in our development processes

Software Development.text: How frequently do you update your environmental impact (or GHG) Inventory with software related Sustainability data?
Software Development.option_a: We don't maintain an inventory covering software development
Software Development.option_b: We don't update the inventory at the moment and/or we don't have specific Sustainability data in the software development team
Software Development.option_c: We provide the Sustainability team with some data as required
Software Development.option_d: We provide annual updates for the environmental impact (or GHG) inventory
Software Development.option_e: We have established an automated connection to upload Sustainability relevant data from the software development teams to the environmental impact (or GHG) inventory

Software Development.text: Are your developers aware of the Scope 3 emissions associated with their development activities?
Software Development.option_a: We don't know what Scope 3 emissions are and/or do not track or monitor them
Software Development.option_b: We are aware of Scope 3 emissions, but do not know how to calculate them
Software Development.option_c: We have a description and basic methodology for calculating and understanding our Scope 3 emissions
Software Development.option_d: We calculate our Scope 3 emissions using models with high level assumptions using estimated data
Software Development.option_e: We calculate and report Scope 3 emissions of our activities using measured (real) data from our activities

Software Development.text: How often does your software development team prepare and share Sustainability reports?
Software Development.option_a: We don't prepare or share Sustainability reports
Software Development.option_b: Reports are prepared annually but rarely shared
Software Development.option_c: Reports are prepared annually and shared with the Sustainability team & senior management
Software Development.option_d: Reports are prepared monthly and shared with the Sustainability team & senior management and all other concerning teams (data centre, hardware & procurement, etc.)
Software Development.option_e: The data is captured continuously and automatically fed into a platform used by the Sustainability team, senior management & related teams

Software Development.text: Do your development teams actively benchmark the quality of your code with regards to Sustainability?
Software Development.option_a: No, we don't benchmark the quality of code from the context of Sustainability
Software Development.option_b: We hire good quality developers and trust that they create a good quality code and that they consider Sustainability
Software Development.option_c: We have communicated several benchmarking studies with our team that specifically list Sustainability metrics
Software Development.option_d: We've benchmarked some of our code in the past and have defined KPIs and targets
Software Development.option_e: Yes, we benchmark the quality of our code on a regular basis (including third party validation) and track this against KPIs and targets that are regularly reviewed

Software Development.text: Are developers trained for efficient code creation, with the aim to ensure that environmental impact is minimised?
Software Development.option_a: No, developers are not trained for efficient code development to minimise environmental impact
Software Development.option_b: Developers are free to use the tools that we have available, but this is not enforced or standardised
Software Development.option_c: Yes, we have development communities that serve the purpose of training with efficiency & Sustainability being included
Software Development.option_d: Yes, our developers receive some training how to code efficiently and have documented standards
Software Development.option_e: Yes, our developers are well trained to create an efficient code, to minimise its impact on the environment. Tooling is deployed to ensure standards are maintained

Software Development.text: Do your developers consider Hardware performance specifications, from the perspective of Sustainability?
Software Development.option_b: For some specific software packages we need to know what hardware we have at our disposal
Software Development.option_c: Yes, we do have conversations with our hardware team on ad hoc basis during the development process
Software Development.option_d: Yes, we have a robust process in place to engage the infrastructure team at key decision points
Software Development.option_e: The data is captured continuously and automatically fed into a Sustainability platform used by the Sustainability team, senior management & related teams

End User Services.text: Who owns the Sustainability agenda for End User Services?
End User Services.option_d: Defined Sustainability role within IT for EUS
End User Services.option_e: Dedicated accountable function, integrated with corporate Sustainability strategy

End User Services.text: Do you have a budget for Sustainability initiatives in EUS?
End User Services.option_d: Defined but limited EUS Sustainability budget
End User Services.option_e: Dedicated recurring budget with measurable ROI in cost & emissions savings

End User Services.text: How are device refresh cycles managed?
End User Services.option_d: Optimised using telemetry and user need
End User Services.option_e: Circular-first: repair/reuse/redeploy/resale default; new only by exception

End User Services.option_c: Option in some categories but not scaled
End User Services.option_d: Widely sourced where viable, supported by policy
End User Services.option_e: Refurb/renew default in procurement; new devices must be justified

End User Services.text: How are device selection standards set?
End User Services.option_d: Standards include embodied emissions, repairability, recyclability
End User Services.option_e: LCA/PCF, right-to-repair, modular design, and emissions thresholds are mandatory

End User Services.text: How do you measure environmental impact of devices?
End User Services.option_d: Device-level telemetry + embodied impact data included
End User Services.option_e: Full lifecycle (embodied + operational + circularity) reported per device, linked to corporate GHG accounting

End User Services.text: How are peripherals (monitors, headsets, keyboards, etc.) managed?
End User Services.option_b: Standard kits defined but no Sustainability filter
End User Services.option_e: Reuse/redeploy default, emissions tracked per peripheral, reported internally
Digital-first strategy: print minimised, emissions tracked and tied to KPIs

End User Services.text: How do you handle end-of-life for EUS equipment?
End User Services.option_d: ITAD partner provides detailed circularity/emissions metrics
End User Services.option_e: Full chain-of-custody with reuse/resale reporting, avoided emissions quantified

End User Services.text: How do you track energy consumption of devices?
End User Services.option_e: Real-time telemetry integrated into Sustainability dashboards and optimisation workflows

End User Services.text: How are mobile devices (phones, tablets) managed?
End User Services.option_d: Lifecycle emissions and repairability factored into procurement
End User Services.option_e: Circular-first sourcing (renew, reuse, modular repair), emissions tracked

End User Services.text: How are accessories (docks, adapters, cables) managed?
End User Services.option_d: Procurement criteria consider lifecycle and reuse
End User Services.option_e: Strict reuse/redeployment enforced; tracked for emissions impact

End User Services.text: Do you measure user behaviour impacts (energy, printing, peripheral use)?
End User Services.option_e: Continuous monitoring with behaviour nudges linked to Sustainability KPIs

End User Services.text: Do EUS Sustainability criteria influence actual procurement decisions?
End User Services.option_d: Regularly considered in procurement alongside cost/performance
End User Services.option_e: Sustainability is a decisive factor — suppliers/products rejected if they fail thresholds

End User Services.text: How is Sustainability data integrated into ITAM/GreenOps reporting?
End User Services.option_e: Fully integrated into ITAM/GreenOps tooling with automated device-level updates

End User Services.text: How aligned is EUS with regulatory standards (UK GBS, EPEAT, CSRD, EU taxonomy)?
End User Services.option_d: Formal alignment for major device categories
End User Services.option_e: Full regulatory compliance embedded; preparing for future rules with reporting and audits
